content: Hey, I'm Frankie, and I'm thinking of planning a trip to Japan. I've always wanted to visit the Tokyo Tower and try some authentic sushi.
That sounds like a great idea! What made you decide to visit Japan now?
Well, I've been working as a Robotics Researcher at MIT, and I needed a break. I recently moved to Raleigh, NC, and I'm loving the food scene here, but I'm craving something new.
I'm sure you'll find plenty of new foods to try in Japan! What kind of research do you do at MIT?
I mostly work on developing new robotics systems for manufacturing. I also like to share my passion for photography and surfing on my free time.
That's really cool! I've always been interested in photography. Do you have a favorite type of camera to use?
Yeah, I love using my DSLR camera! It's so much fun to capture the beauty of the world around me. By the way, have you ever been to the mountains in Japan? I've heard they're breathtaking.
Yes, I've heard great things about them too! You should definitely check them out. What do you think about trying some mountain biking while you're in Japan?
Actually, I've always wanted to try it! Maybe I'll find a new hobby. My friend, Kenji, recommended this one trail that's supposed to be amazing.
That sounds like a lot of fun! I'm sure you'll have a great time. What's your favorite type of Japanese food to try?
I love trying new types of sushi! I've been wanting to try some authentic ramen and udon noodles.
That sounds delicious! I'm sure you'll find plenty of great restaurants to try. Do you have any plans to visit any of the famous temples or shrines in Japan?

Yeah, definitely! I want to visit the Fushimi Inari shrine and try to hike the famous orange torii gate trail. I've also been wanting to visit the Kiyomizu-dera temple and try some of the local green tea.
That sounds like a great plan! I'm sure you'll have a wonderful time. What's your favorite type of music to listen to while you're traveling?
I love listening to J-pop! It's so upbeat and fun. I've been listening to a lot of Arashi lately.
That's great! I'm sure it will be a good accompaniment to your travels. Have you thought about visiting any of the local festivals or events while you're in Japan?
Actually, I've been thinking about visiting the Cherry Blossom Festival. I've heard it's an amazing event with so much to see and do.
That's a great choice! I'm sure you'll have a fantastic time. Don't forget to try some of the local sake while you're there!

Will do! Thanks for chatting with me, I'm feeling more excited than ever for my trip. It's going to be on October 1st, and I'll be there for 12 days. My border collie, Ziggy, is going to stay with my friend, Sarah, while I'm away.
